best_edges.evidence: pullback_long is the largest real edge: 31 trades, $2419.4 PnL, $252.9/hour, 100% win rate, no slow >=12h trades. Tail is material but not portfolio worst: worst MAE -403.2, avg MAE -110.4.

charm_pin_pullback_long contributes $1077.4 over 29 trades with median hold 2.97 minutes, avg MAE -57.8, worst MAE -335.75, and $208.9/hour. Extended-VWAP winners are a known protected set, so broad extension pruning is dangerous.
breakout_long_1dte contributes $1315.3 over 20 VALID_1DTE_ONLY trades, no slow trades, worst MAE -386.75. Prior direct 1DTE tightening reduced score and net profit, so keep baseline behavior stable.
charm_wall_breakaway_long has only 5 trades but $987.0 PnL, $234.7/hour, median MFE 150.5, and no slow trades. Prior exit compression hurt objective, implying this profile needs convex exits.

best_edges.edge: Squeeze continuation with strong option context
best_edges.evidence: squeeze_long produced 10 trades, $723.0 PnL, worst MAE -214.2, no slow trades. It is not the top efficiency edge but has contained tail risk versus larger profiles.

broken_edges.evidence: call_wall_acceptance_long has only 2 trades and high standalone efficiency, but owns the global worst MAE: -655.2, avg MAE -333.7, median hold 66.2 minutes. Worst tail still made $546, so blunt blocking sacrifices a major winner.
STALE_0DTE fallback produced only $162.8 over 5 trades. breakout_long_no_0dte stale subset had 2 trades, $71.0 PnL, $40.5/hour, avg MAE -210.6, and median hold 52.6 minutes.
breakout_long, wall_reclaim_long, wall_breakout_retest_long, late_day_pin_fade, negative_gamma_momentum_long, and trend_day_pullback_long are routed but disabled or zero-trade in this sample.
broken_edges.problem: No current evidence of live edge

broken_edges.evidence: Runtime-risk exits improved worst MAE in one experiment but hurt objective heavily; EMA period and second-resolution fallback changes created catastrophic slow-tail/drawdown branches.

market_regime_read: Short, high-win-rate, take-profit-only sample over 12.3 calendar days. Realized loss rate is 0% and slow >=12h trades are 0, so the risk-reducer problem is hidden MAE and capital tie-up, not realized losses. run_044 is the net-profit and score leader, but it worsened worst MAE to -655.2 and drawdown to 0.007 versus the robust leader family at -513.4 MAE and 0.004 drawdown. The current leader added profit through a convex call-wall/extended-VWAP tail, but the same branch introduced the worst underwater path. Risk work should be surgical and profile-aware, not broad pruning.

new_strategy_ideas.edge: Path behavior separates recoveries from clean winners better than static entry features.
new_strategy_ideas.failure_mode: Could overfit delayed recoveries and block profitable convex winners unless validated on protected fast-winner UTCs and out-of-sample days.
new_strategy_ideas.name: Early Progress Heat Classifier
new_strategy_ideas.required_data: Intratrade path samples: first profit time, first 15m MFE/MAE, MFE-before-MAE, half-TP touch, profile, option data state.
new_strategy_ideas.trigger: Long entry fails to make meaningful positive progress before early MAE expansion, especially in pullback_long, call_wall_acceptance_long, and charm_pin_pullback_long.
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: Worst MAE improves toward <= -513.4, average MAE improves, protected fast-winner retention >=95%, total net profit >=98% of run_044, slow >=12h remains 0.

new_strategy_ideas.edge: 1DTE fallback is profitable but may contain breakout and pullback/reclaim subregimes.
new_strategy_ideas.failure_mode: Router overlap may cannibalize breakout_long_1dte or companion profile may be unreachable.
new_strategy_ideas.name: 1DTE Pullback Companion Split
new_strategy_ideas.required_data: VALID_1DTE_ONLY trades with ES reclaim/pullback features, option-map state, and existing breakout_long_1dte attribution.
new_strategy_ideas.trigger: 0DTE unavailable, 1DTE map valid, and ES state resembles pullback/reclaim rather than clean breakout continuation.
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: Combined 1DTE PnL >= $1315.3, 1DTE average MAE improves from -124.6, worst MAE no worse than -386.75, total score flat or better.

new_strategy_ideas.edge: Dormant pin-avoid concepts may separate charm/pin tail states without weakening current charm-pin edge.
new_strategy_ideas.failure_mode: Can cannibalize charm_pin_pullback_long or charm_wall_breakaway_long without adding net edge.
new_strategy_ideas.name: Dormant Charm-Wall Pin-Avoid Attribution Probe
new_strategy_ideas.required_data: Router attribution, dormant profile standalone PnL, combined charm/pin MAE, protected extended-VWAP winner retention.
new_strategy_ideas.trigger: VALID_0DTE charm/pin environment where existing charm_pin_pullback_long or charm_wall_breakaway_long would otherwise take high-extension tail exposure.
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: Activated dormant profile positive standalone PnL or lower combined charm/pin worst MAE, total net profit >=98% of run_044, protected fast-winner retention >=95%.

new_strategy_ideas.edge: Call-wall acceptance has convex payoff but unacceptable static MAE in current form.
new_strategy_ideas.failure_mode: A static filter may remove the $546 worst-tail winner and destroy most profile PnL.
new_strategy_ideas.name: Call-Wall Convex Recovery Monitor
new_strategy_ideas.required_data: Call-wall target distance, forward DEX, strike acceptance, intratrade progress, ES VWAP extension, TP-touch path.
new_strategy_ideas.trigger: call_wall_acceptance_long enters with very wide target and extended ES-VWAP while early path stalls or fails half-TP follow-through.
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: call_wall_acceptance_long worst MAE improves materially while retaining >=70% of its PnL and total portfolio score does not degrade.

next_experiments.config_bias: Study small boundary nudges around the already-enabled extended-VWAP rejection family. Avoid global ES-VWAP caps or setup floors because extended-VWAP winners contribute $1051.9 and protected fast winners contribute $4818.1.
next_experiments.goal: Reduce hidden MAE without pruning the extended-VWAP edge.
next_experiments.hypothesis: The six deep-MAE near misses are close to current veto thresholds; a narrow threshold study can catch some tail rows while preserving protected winners.
next_experiments.name: Extended-VWAP Near-Miss Boundary Retest
next_experiments.primary_metric: Worst MAE and retained protected extended-VWAP winners; secondary total PnL >=98% of run_044.
next_experiments.risk: The previous max-VWAP-distance nudge improved profit but worsened worst MAE, so over-widening this family may admit or block the wrong convex states.

next_experiments.config_bias: Do not disable call_wall_acceptance_long outright. Test a narrow profile-local risk box around extended ES-VWAP, low ADX, weak/negative VWAP acceleration, wide target distance, and long expected recovery state.
next_experiments.goal: Keep the call-wall convex winner while reducing its -655.2 underwater tail.
next_experiments.hypothesis: The worst tail is a call-wall acceptance outlier with ES-VWAP 28.76, ADX 14.45, negative acceleration, and 120-minute TP path; profile-local admission/risk boundaries may separate this from cleaner call-wall setups.
next_experiments.name: Call-Wall Tail Containment Holdout
next_experiments.primary_metric: Portfolio worst MAE, call_wall_acceptance_long PnL retention, and total drawdown.
next_experiments.risk: Only 2 trades exist; blocking one trade can falsely look like risk reduction while removing 8.5% of total PnL.

next_experiments.config_bias: Focus on pullback states with weak early progress, low setup quality, weak structure, and long hold buckets. Avoid broad pullback_long TP expansion or ES-VWAP max-distance relaxation, which previously hurt objective.
next_experiments.goal: Lower pullback_long -403.2 worst MAE while preserving its $2419.4 core edge.
next_experiments.hypothesis: The pullback tail row had low setup quality 46.36, structure score 3, negative higher-low/high distances, ADX slope -1.31, no positive before MAE, and 155-minute hold; this is a weak-structure recovery state, not the median pullback edge.
next_experiments.name: Pullback Weak-Structure Tail Holdout
next_experiments.primary_metric: pullback_long worst MAE and PnL retention >=90%; total net profit >=98% of run_044.
next_experiments.risk: Good winners also include weak structure and low setup quality, so single-feature pruning is likely overbroad.

next_experiments.config_bias: Keep breakout_long_1dte thresholds stable. If optimizer supports it, compare a router companion split with pullback_long_1dte against baseline; otherwise freeze 1DTE.
next_experiments.goal: Improve 1DTE risk-adjusted behavior without repeating failed direct tightening.
next_experiments.hypothesis: VALID_1DTE_ONLY contributes $1315.3 but has avg MAE -124.6; a companion split may quarantine pullback-like 1DTE states better than tightening breakout_long_1dte.
next_experiments.name: 1DTE Companion Split Validation
next_experiments.primary_metric: Combined 1DTE PnL, 1DTE average/worst MAE, and breakout_long_1dte retained PnL.
next_experiments.risk: Companion routing can cannibalize profitable breakout fallback trades.

next_experiments.config_bias: Isolate no-0DTE stale fallback behavior only. Preserve VALID_0DTE routing and the single EXPIRED_0DTE winner.
next_experiments.goal: Reduce low-efficiency fallback exposure without touching core 0DTE edge.
next_experiments.hypothesis: STALE_0DTE trades add only $162.8 and include low PnL/hour, long-hold breakout fallback behavior; mild stale-specific quality constraints may improve drawdown efficiency.
next_experiments.name: Stale No-0DTE Fallback Efficiency Holdout
next_experiments.primary_metric: Fallback PnL/hour, fallback worst MAE, unchanged VALID_0DTE PnL/trades, total PnL >=98% of run_044.
next_experiments.risk: Tiny 5-trade sample; stale filtering can remove useful insurance or distort data-state attribution.

next_experiments.config_bias: Freeze exits and preserve require-breakout. If tested, only examine admission geometry that reduces tail without compressing the 12-point convex target.
next_experiments.goal: Maintain charm-wall PnL while preventing hidden MAE growth.
next_experiments.hypothesis: charm_wall_breakaway_long remains efficient, but one bad-MAE representative overlaps fast-winner protection; exit compression has already hurt, so only admission-quality holdouts are worth testing.
next_experiments.name: Charm-Wall Admission Geometry Risk Check
next_experiments.primary_metric: charm_wall_breakaway_long PnL/hour >=225, worst MAE no worse than -357, total PnL >=98% of run_044.
next_experiments.risk: Small sample and protected-winner overlap make overfitting likely.

research_notes: Do not emit broad config pruning from the hidden signature table: the best category signatures retain only 80-83% PnL and still leave worst MAE -655.2. The useful research target is the mismatch between run_044’s higher net profit and worse MAE versus run_029/run_037/run_038/run_040 robust leaders. The incremental profit came with a worse tail, so next experiments should be evaluated against both run_044 and the robust family baseline: retain most of $7532.8 while moving worst MAE back toward <= -513.4 and drawdown toward 0.004. Avoid EMA period, indicator resolution, runtime-risk exits, and broad 1DTE tightening; all have negative or mixed memory.
risk_controls: Promotion threshold should require total net profit >=98% of run_044 unless worst MAE improves materially below -513.4.
Reject any risk reducer that blocks more than 5% of protected fast-winner UTCs or more than 10% of completed trades without major MAE improvement.
Keep slow >=12h trades at 0; any reappearance is an immediate rejection signal.
Compare against robust leader family, not only run_044: target worst MAE <= -513.4 and drawdown near 0.004 while preserving most of run_044 profit.
Do not promote changes that improve average MAE while leaving global worst MAE at -655.2 unless PnL and drawdown improve enough to justify it.
Profile-local tests should report standalone PnL, profile trade count, worst MAE, avg MAE, and cannibalization of neighboring profiles.
